title: equiv_opt falsely claims equivalence on designs with different unknown modules
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.51+41 (git sha1 0c689091e, ccache clang++ 16.0.6 -Og -fPIC)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

```
module gold(input X, output Y);
    bar bar(X, Y);
endmodule

module gate(input X, output Y);
    baz baz(X, Y);
endmodule
```

`read_verilog bug.v; proc; equiv_make gold gate equiv; equiv_induct equiv; equiv_status -assert equiv`

### Expected Behavior

Failure to prove equivalence or other error

### Actual Behavior

Only warnings are emitted and equivalence is claimed to be proven:
```
3. Executing EQUIV_INDUCT pass.
Found 1 unproven $equiv cells in module equiv:
Warning: No SAT model available for cell baz_gate (baz).
Warning: No SAT model available for cell bar_gold (bar).
  Proving existence of base case for step 1. (10 clauses over 5 variables)
  Proving induction step 1. (17 clauses over 8 variables)
  Proof for induction step holds. Entire workset of 1 cells proven!
Proved 1 previously unproven $equiv cells.

4. Executing EQUIV_STATUS pass.
Found 1 $equiv cells in equiv:
  Of those cells 1 are proven and 0 are unproven.
  Equivalence successfully proven!
```
code_blocks:
3. Executing EQUIV_INDUCT pass.
Found 1 unproven $equiv cells in module equiv:
Warning: No SAT model available for cell baz_gate (baz).
Warning: No SAT model available for cell bar_gold (bar).
  Proving existence of base case for step 1. (10 clauses over 5 variables)
  Proving induction step 1. (17 clauses over 8 variables)
  Proof for induction step holds. Entire workset of 1 cells proven!
Proved 1 previously unproven $equiv cells.

4. Executing EQUIV_STATUS pass.
Found 1 $equiv cells in equiv:
  Of those cells 1 are proven and 0 are unproven.
  Equivalence successfully proven!
